aliases: 鮭の握り寿司 (Sake no nigirizushi)
三文鱼握寿司 (Sānwènyú wò shòusī)

chinese: 三文鱼握寿司 (Sānwènyú wò shòusī)
japanese: 鮭の握り寿司 (Sake no nigirizushi)

region_of_origin: Japan (Edo-mae sushi style, Tokyo region); widely served in Singapore at Sushi Tei, Genki Sushi, Sakae Sushi, Ichiban Boshi
food_subgroup: Sushi — nigiri; sushi rice (seasoned with rice vinegar, sugar, salt) topped with raw farmed Atlantic salmon slice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Salmon Nigiri Sushi as a prepared restaurant dish. Closest parent would be a generic sushi food product node; not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0JQA used as closest parent for nigiri sushi with fish topping; no exact code for salmon nigiri specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for salmon nigiri sushi as a prepared food item.

main_ingredients: sushi rice (Japanese short-grain rice, rice vinegar, sugar, salt)
allergens: gluten (soy sauce if dipped — not included in this record)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of salmon nigiri sushi is 2 pieces, weighing approximately 90g total (sushi rice ~36g + raw Atlantic salmon ~40g + trace wasabi and pickled ginger); soy sauce for dipping is not included in this nutritional record.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, raw (FDC ID 175167) combined with sushi rice calculation derived from USDA FDC cooked Japanese short-grain rice (FDC ID 169756) with standard sushi seasoning ratios
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Composite record: farmed Atlantic salmon (USDA FDC 175167) at ~44% weight fraction and seasoned sushi rice (USDA FDC 169756 + standard sushi vinegar seasoning: rice vinegar, sugar, salt at ~8g sugar per 100g cooked rice) at ~40% weight fraction, with trace wasabi/gari at ~16%. Per-100g values calculated by weighted blend. Soy sauce dipping not included. Japan MEXT database was not used as primary source per approved source policy; USDA FDC used as approved equivalent.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — sushi rice / Japanese short-grain rice analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~62 estimated for sushi rice based on University of Sydney GI Database reference for Japanese short-grain rice (GI ~64, glucose reference) adjusted downward slightly for the fat-moderating effect of Atlantic salmon (~7.8g fat per 100g composite) and adjusted upward for added sugar in sushi rice seasoning. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (62 × 18g) / 100 ≈ 11.2, rounded to 10.5 per input record. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from seasoned sushi rice (~272mg/100g); soy sauce dipping substantially increases sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Two pieces of salmon nigiri without soy sauce contribute approximately 245mg sodium; patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit soy sauce dipping, as full dipping of a 6-piece order can raise total sodium intake to 1,100–1,300mg from sushi alone.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sushi rice GI ~62 (medium); added sugar in sushi rice seasoning (~4g sugar per 100g composite); GL ~10.5 per 2-piece serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Salmon nigiri has a medium GI (~62) and a moderate glycemic load (~10.5 per 2 pieces); patients with type 2 diabetes should be counselled that sushi rice contains added sugar and is not a low-carbohydrate food, and should limit portions to 2–4 pieces per meal while monitoring postprandial glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 fatty acid content (EPA+DHA ~0.8g per 2-piece serving) from farmed Atlantic salmon; low saturated fat (~1.5g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon nigiri is a cardioprotective choice due to its high EPA+DHA content (~0.8g per 2 pieces; ~2.4g per 6-piece order), which supports triglyceride reduction and anti-inflammatory pathways; saturated fat is low at 1.5g/100g, making it suitable for cardiovascular disease patients when soy sauce dipping is minimised to control sodium.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA+DHA omega-3 content from farmed Atlantic salmon
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: A 6-piece order (~270g) delivers approximately 2.4g EPA+DHA, approaching the 3g/day therapeutic anti-inflammatory dose; salmon nigiri is one of the most omega-3-efficient Japanese restaurant foods per calorie.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon Nigiri Sushi (2 pieces, ~90g) is a classic Japanese Edo-mae sushi dish consisting of two hand-pressed ovals of seasoned sushi rice (~18g each) topped with a thin slice of raw farmed Atlantic salmon (~20g each), with trace wasabi between rice and fish. The rice is seasoned with rice vinegar, sugar, and salt — a critical clinical point as sushi rice is NOT plain rice and contains added sugar (~4g sugar per 100g composite). Per 2-piece serving: approximately 175 kcal, 9g protein, 7g fat (including ~0.8g EPA+DHA omega-3), 18g carbohydrate, ~245mg sodium (soy sauce dipping NOT included). GI ~62 (medium), GL ~10.5. Texture: soft, slightly sticky rice with silky, buttery raw salmon. Widely available in Singapore at Sushi Tei, Genki Sushi, Sakae Sushi, and Ichiban Boshi. Halal, gluten-free, dairy-free. Key clinical notes: (1) Soy sauce dipping adds 100–400mg sodium per dipping session — hypertension patients must be counselled. (2) Sushi rice contains added sugar — type 2 diabetes patients who believe sushi is sugar-free must be corrected. (3) A 6-piece order delivers ~2.4g EPA+DHA, approaching therapeutic anti-inflammatory omega-3 dose. Source: USDA FDC 175167 (farmed Atlantic salmon) + USDA FDC 169756 (Japanese short-grain rice) with standard sushi seasoning calculation. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 175167 (farmed Atlantic salmon, exact match) and USDA FDC 169756 (Japanese short-grain rice, exact match) with standard sushi seasoning ratios applied. Composite calculation is well-supported. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous reference for Japanese short-grain rice with documented adjustment rationale. All approved sources used.
notes: water_g set to -1: moisture data for the composite sushi rice + salmon dish is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation; individual component moisture values could be used but the composite figure is not reliably calculable without validated recipe weights. USDA FDC was consulted and does not provide a composite moisture value for this dish. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.8g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.9g) are estimated from USDA FDC 175167 fatty acid profile for farmed Atlantic salmon scaled to the composite dish weight fraction; these are approximations. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible for raw farmed Atlantic salmon and plain sushi rice per USDA FDC. Soy sauce for dipping is NOT included in this nutritional record; sodium values reflect only the dish as served. Sushi rice contains added sugar (~4g/100g composite) — this is a critical patient education point for diabetes management. GI source is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference for Japanese short-grain rice (GI ~64) adjusted for salmon fat moderating effect and sushi sugar addition; confidence 0.9. Japan MEXT 8th Edition database was referenced in the input record but is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2 source policy; USDA FDC was used as the approved equivalent. FoodEx2 code A0JQA used as closest parent; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for salmon nigiri specifically. This dish is Japanese in origin but is widely served in Singapore; local-language names in Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as transliterations reflecting Singapore's multilingual context.
